description: Add attempt.committed event type with Zod schema to shared/events.ts
steps_to_verify: attempt.committed event type exists in shared/events.ts with payload { attempt_id: string, commit_sha: string, empty: boolean }
Zod schema validates the payload shape correctly
Event is included in PROJECTION_SUBSCRIPTIONS for the attempt projection

description: Add phase.diff_snapshotted event type with Zod schema to shared/events.ts
steps_to_verify: phase.diff_snapshotted event type exists with payload { attempt_id: string, phase_name: string, diff_hash: string, base_sha: string }
Zod schema validates the payload shape correctly
Event is included in PROJECTION_SUBSCRIPTIONS for the attempt projection

description: Update task.worktree_created event payload to include base_sha alongside base_ref
steps_to_verify: task.worktree_created payload includes base_sha: string (resolved commit SHA)
base_ref: string (symbolic reference) is preserved unchanged
Zod schema updated to require base_sha

description: Resolve base_ref to base_sha at worktree creation time via git rev-parse
steps_to_verify: createWorktree runs git rev-parse <base_ref> and stores the result as base_sha
task.worktree_created event emitted with both base_ref and base_sha
base_sha is a full 40-character commit SHA, not a symbolic ref

description: Move commit from per-phase to per-attempt, firing after all phases/gates/auditor complete
steps_to_verify: Existing per-phase commit logic (step 3b) is removed from the phase loop
A single commit happens after the phase loop completes, still within runAttempt
Commit message follows format: Attempt <N> of <task_id> — <outcome> with config hash, cost, and duration in body
git commit --allow-empty is used when no file changes were produced

description: Emit attempt.committed event after the per-attempt commit with commit SHA and empty flag
steps_to_verify: attempt.committed event is emitted after the git commit succeeds
commit_sha field contains the actual SHA from the commit
empty field is true when --allow-empty was used (no file changes), false otherwise

description: Anchor per-phase diff capture to base_sha (attempt 1) or previous attempt commit SHA (attempt 2+)
steps_to_verify: Attempt 1 diff uses base_sha from task.worktree_created as the diff base
Attempt 2+ diff uses the previous attempt's commit_sha from attempt.committed as the diff base
phase.diff_snapshotted event is emitted with the diff_hash and base_sha
Diff blob is stored in the blob store

description: Keep diff_hash on phase.completed populated from the snapshotted blob — do not remove the field
steps_to_verify: phase.completed still includes diff_hash field
diff_hash value matches the hash from the corresponding phase.diff_snapshotted event
Review UI continues to work unchanged reading diff_hash from phase.completed

description: Change no_changes outcome status from draft to awaiting_review
steps_to_verify: When finalOutcome is no_changes, newStatus is set to awaiting_review instead of draft
Task appears in the awaiting_review state in the sidebar after a no-changes attempt
User can approve, reject, or retry from the awaiting_review state

description: Compute effective_diff_attempt_id on the attempt projection for empty-attempt fallback
steps_to_verify: Non-empty attempts have effective_diff_attempt_id equal to their own attempt_id
Empty attempts walk back through previous_attempt_id to find the most recent non-empty attempt
If no prior non-empty attempt exists, effective_diff_attempt_id is null
Field is computed in the projection reducer, not in the UI

description: Retry attempt starts from prior attempt's commit with delta-only diff semantics
steps_to_verify: Attempt 2 runs in a worktree where HEAD is attempt 1's commit
Retry prompt references git diff HEAD~1 HEAD as the previous attempt's changes
Auditor concerns from the prior attempt are included in the retry prompt
git diff HEAD after attempt 2 shows only attempt 2's delta, not cumulative changes

description: Display empty-attempt banner using effective_diff_attempt_id with fallback to prior attempt diff
steps_to_verify: Review UI reads effective_diff_attempt_id to fetch the diff blob
When current attempt id differs from effective_diff_attempt_id, a banner renders: 'This attempt made no changes. Showing diff from attempt <M>.'
When effective_diff_attempt_id is null, renders: 'No attempts have produced changes yet.'
When attempt is non-empty, no banner is shown and diff displays normally

description: Discard uncommitted worktree changes on server restart via git reset --hard HEAD
steps_to_verify: On server startup, all known worktree paths are checked for uncommitted changes
Any uncommitted changes are discarded with git reset --hard HEAD
Committed state (completed attempts) is preserved
Recovery runs before any new attempts are started

description: Update existing no_changes test to expect awaiting_review instead of draft
steps_to_verify: phaseRunner.test.ts no_changes test asserts status is awaiting_review
Test passes with the updated assertion

description: Add tests for per-attempt commit lifecycle
steps_to_verify: Test that a successful attempt produces a git commit with the correct message format
Test that an empty attempt uses --allow-empty and emits attempt.committed with empty: true
Test that attempt.committed event contains the correct commit_sha
Test that no commit happens during the phase loop (only after all phases complete)

description: Add tests for anchored diff capture with base_sha
steps_to_verify: Test that attempt 1 diff uses base_sha from worktree creation as the base
Test that attempt 2 diff uses attempt 1's commit_sha as the base
Test that phase.diff_snapshotted event is emitted with correct diff_hash and base_sha
Test that non-empty attempt sets effective_diff_attempt_id to its own id
Test that empty attempt walks back to find the most recent non-empty attempt
Test that effective_diff_attempt_id is null when no prior non-empty attempt exists
